`,(0,e.jsx)(s.p,{children:"The output of the model is a set of ranked images that are similar to the query image. As a result, visual search systems can be framed as a ranking problem. In general, the goal of ranking problems is to rank a collection of items, such as images, websites, products, etc., based on their relevance to a query, so that more relevant items appear higher in the search results. Many ML applications, such as recommendation systems, search engines, document retrieval, and online advertising, can be framed as ranking problems. In this chapter, we will use a widely-used approach called representation learning. Let\u2019s examine this in more detail."}),`
